Midewin_Diversity_Comp.Results.Figures

Marissa Zaricor

Last Updated: 2026-06-05

Set up and Overview

Running Title: Management modifies consumer top-down control of plant diversity in grassland restoration

Background: Quantifying the independent and interactive effects of management (fire, mow, herbicide, none) and consumers (bison, small mammals, no mammals) on plant community composition and diversity (species richness, Shannon diversity, and evenness) during tallgrass prairie restoration

Author: Marissa Zaricor

Co-authors: Lauren Sullivan

Created: 2025-05-16

Files:

  1. Midewin_cover_clean.csv - contains plant cover, codes, taxon, etc.

  2. Midewin_Aug-Aug_Grazing_Influence_22_24.csv- bison raster data for total (sum by year) and weighted total (sum of weighted by month/season) but aug year-1 to aug year current. (last updated Jan 2025)

Last Updated: 2026-06-05

Libraries, Files, Setup

Code for ggplot themes for publications

## set working directory to source file location

cover_clean<- read.csv("Midewin_cover_clean.csv")
cover_clean$herbivory_trt<-factor(cover_clean$herbivory_trt, levels=c("XX", "SX", "SB")) #relevel herbivory


graze_Aug <-read_csv("Midewin_Aug-Aug_Grazing_Influence_22-24.csv")

Species Richness

1. Calculate species richness

#calculate species richness 
specRich1<- cover_clean %>% 
  filter(live==1) %>%  # remove anything not a plant
  filter(unknown==0) %>%  # remove any unknowns
  filter(code !="" & !is.na(code)) %>%  #remove any empty rows in  code column
  group_by(year, block, plot, management_trt, herbivory_trt, bison_pasture) %>%  #do not include other columns here for now
  summarise(richness=n_distinct(code), .groups = "drop") #counts all distinct species per plot

2. Add in bison covariate data

3. Fit the model

#Main results 2024 so remove east and other years
rich_W24<- specRich1 %>% filter(year=="2024") 



#fitting the model, guassian 
glmm_richW24<-glmmTMB(richness~herbivory_trt*management_trt+(1|block/plot), data = rich_W24, family = gaussian)
summary(glmm_richW24)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          richness ~ herbivory_trt * management_trt + (1 | block/plot)
## Data: rich_W24
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     321.9     356.0    -145.9     291.9        57 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  plot:block (Intercept) 1.3530   1.1632  
##  block      (Intercept) 0.8449   0.9192  
##  Residual               1.3530   1.1632  
## Number of obs: 72, groups:  plot:block, 72; block, 24
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 1.35 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)                               6.0000     0.7693   7.799 6.23e-15
## herbivory_trtSX                           0.5000     0.9497   0.526 0.598566
## herbivory_trtSB                           3.3333     0.9497   3.510 0.000449
## management_trtfire                        4.0000     1.0880   3.677 0.000236
## management_trtherbicide                   2.5000     1.0880   2.298 0.021568
## management_trtmow                         4.0000     1.0880   3.677 0.000236
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtfire       -1.0000     1.3431  -0.745 0.456559
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtfire       -1.0000     1.3431  -0.745 0.456556
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtherbicide   1.1667     1.3431   0.869 0.385062
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtherbicide  -2.6667     1.3431  -1.985 0.047099
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtmow         0.6667     1.3431   0.496 0.619647
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtmow        -2.3333     1.3431  -1.737 0.082347
##                                            
## (Intercept)                             ***
## herbivory_trtSX                            
## herbivory_trtSB                         ***
## management_trtfire                      ***
## management_trtherbicide                 *  
## management_trtmow                       ***
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtfire         
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtfire         
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtherbicide    
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtherbicide *  
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtmow          
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtmow       .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#bison covariate is not significant and will be removed from the model

#Results: significnat interaction and main effects 

#AIC 321

#use wald chi sqaaure tests to show our approx. entire effects, using type = III because better for our interactions structure
anova_w24<-Anova(glmm_richW24, type="III")
anova_w24
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type III Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: richness
##                               Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## (Intercept)                  60.829  1  6.225e-15 ***
## herbivory_trt                14.330  2  0.0007731 ***
## management_trt               18.059  3  0.0004278 ***
## herbivory_trt:management_trt 14.197  6  0.0275144 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Check assumptions

## $uniformity
## 
##  Asymptotic one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  simulationOutput$scaledResiduals
## D = 0.16256, p-value = 0.04451
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
## 
## 
## $dispersion
## 
##  DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs.
##  simulated
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## dispersion = 1.2844, p-value = 0.088
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
## 
## 
## $outliers
## 
##  DHARMa outlier test based on exact binomial test with approximate
##  expectations
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## outliers at both margin(s) = 0, observations = 72, p-value = 1
## alternative hypothesis: true probability of success is not equal to 0.007968127
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.00000000 0.04994408
## sample estimates:
## frequency of outliers (expected: 0.00796812749003984 ) 
##                                                      0
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: 0.743
##      Marginal R2: 0.583

Richness Delta Model (23-24)

Calculate the difference from 2023 to 2024 in specrich

#filter data to only bison pasture and years 23-24
rich23_24<-specRich1 %>% filter(bison_pasture=="yes", year %in% c("2023", "2024"))


# rearrange and calculcate difference between years
rich_diff <- rich23_24 %>% 
  arrange(block, plot, year) %>% #makes sure 2023 is before our 2024 rows for each plot
  group_by(block, plot, management_trt, herbivory_trt) %>% #keeps our unique combos
  filter(n() == 2) %>% #only plots with data in both years are used
  mutate(diff_richness = richness - lag(richness)) %>% #this calculates our differences
  filter(!is.na(diff_richness)) %>% ungroup #remove any nas

Fit the model

diff_glmm1<- glmmTMB(diff_richness~herbivory_trt*management_trt+ (1|block/plot), data=rich_diff, family = gaussian)
summary(diff_glmm1)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          
## diff_richness ~ herbivory_trt * management_trt + (1 | block/plot)
## Data: rich_diff
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     310.8     343.2    -140.4     280.8        49 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  plot:block (Intercept) 1.589    1.261   
##  block      (Intercept) 2.354    1.534   
##  Residual               1.589    1.261   
## Number of obs: 64, groups:  plot:block, 64; block, 22
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 1.59 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept)                             -2.19999    1.05189  -2.091   0.0365 * 
## herbivory_trtSX                          1.40000    1.12756   1.242   0.2144   
## herbivory_trtSB                          3.00001    1.12756   2.661   0.0078 **
## management_trtfire                      -0.80000    1.42427  -0.562   0.5743   
## management_trtherbicide                 -0.09848    1.47323  -0.067   0.9467   
## management_trtmow                        2.19998    1.48760   1.479   0.1392   
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtfire       0.41989    1.57114   0.267   0.7893   
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtfire       0.66666    1.52672   0.437   0.6624   
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtherbicide -0.26818    1.57250  -0.170   0.8646   
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtherbicide -0.53486    1.57250  -0.340   0.7338   
## herbivory_trtSX:management_trtmow       -1.59999    1.59461  -1.003   0.3157   
## herbivory_trtSB:management_trtmow       -2.60000    1.59461  -1.631   0.1030   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova_diff<-Anova(diff_glmm1, type="III")
#bison covariate was not significant and dropped from the model AIC 312
#only herbivory trt significant (p=0.028)

Check assumptions

Shannon and Evenness Metrics

Data Cleaning and Set up

Calculate shannon and evennes metrics

Shannon Diversity 2024

Fit model for Shannon diversrity

##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          shannon ~ herbivory_trt * management_trt + (1 | block/plot)
## Data: shannon_W24
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##        NA        NA        NA        NA        57 
##

##                           Term     Chisq Df      p_value
## 1                  (Intercept) 165.93090  1 5.727432e-38

## dispersion = 1.2131, p-value = 0.144
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
##

##  simulation under H0 = fitted model
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## ratioObsSim = NaN, p-value = 1
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided

Same as above. Also beta model reinforces that the single coefficient is non-significant here. Will still use guassian model for results
